action.hypothesis: The bucketed locality bias that improved both K=16 and K=32 may also create a better mid-low-K tradeoff at K=24 under EF=16 by preserving the same structured competition pattern in the gap between those two new wins.
action.summary: The bucketed family has now produced real frontier gains at both K=16 and K=32, so the highest-value next step is to interpolate the missing K=24 slot rather than jumping to a larger K. This keeps architecture and recipe fixed, changes only K, and directly tests whether the new family owns a broader contiguous low-K region instead of just two isolated points.
action.self_review: This is the cleanest frontier-mapping follow-up after back-to-back improvements: probe the missing interior K value with the same recipe. The main risk is that the bucketed advantage is non-monotonic across K, but K=24 is exactly the right place to resolve that uncertainty while prioritizing smaller K.
notes_to_memory: Round 127 maps the interior K=24 point after the bucketed family improved the frontier at both K=16 and K=32 under EF=16.
Hold architecture and recipe fixed so the result isolates the K-axis and determines whether bucketed locality yields a contiguous low-K frontier segment.
K=24 is higher value than pushing to larger K because smaller-K non-dominated tradeoffs remain the main objective.
next_hypotheses: If K=24 is competitive, continue mapping the bucketed family at K=64 to test how far the benefit extends into the higher-K lane.
If K=24 underperforms while K=16 and K=32 stay strong, treat the bucketed family as having a non-monotonic sweet spot and confirm with a repeat only if the miss is small.
If K=24 improves clearly, consider promoting the bucketed family as the default EF=16 backbone for the low-to-mid-K regime.

This keeps architecture and recipe fixed, changes only K, and directly tests whether the new family owns a broader contiguous low-K region instead of just two isolated points.

The main risk is that the bucketed advantage is non-monotonic across K, but K=24 is exactly the right place to resolve that uncertainty while prioritizing smaller K.
